channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. B
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review B
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org

item.title: Interacting ground states of moiré ladders
item.description:
Author(s): Paban Kumar Patra, Ranjith R. Kumar, Yixuan Huang, and Hridis K. Pal

Moiré materials have emerged as a rich platform for exploring strong correlation effects in low dimensions, with twisted bilayer graphene (TBG) as a paradigmatic example. To distill the essential ingredients driving moiré-induced phases, a simplified one-dimensional analog—a two-leg ladder with spat…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104432] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Paban Kumar Patra, Ranjith R. Kumar, Yixuan Huang, and Hridis K. Pal

Moiré materials have emerged as a rich platform for exploring strong correlation effects in low dimensions, with twisted bilayer graphene (TBG) as a paradigmatic example. To distill the essential ingredients driving moiré-induced phases, a simplified one-dimensional analog—a two-leg ladder with spat…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104432] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026
item.dc:title: Interacting ground states of moiré ladders
item.dc:creator: Paban Kumar Patra, Ranjith R. Kumar, Yixuan Huang, and Hridis K. Pal
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Non-Hermitian quantum geometric tensor and nonlinear electrical response
item.description:
Author(s): Kai Chen and Jie Zhu

We demonstrate that the non-Hermitian quantum geometric tensor (QGT) governs nonlinear electrical responses in systems with a spectral line gap. The quantum metric, which is the symmetric component of the QGT and takes complex values in non-Hermitian systems, generates an intrinsic nonlinear conduct…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115135] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Kai Chen and Jie Zhu

We demonstrate that the non-Hermitian quantum geometric tensor (QGT) governs nonlinear electrical responses in systems with a spectral line gap. The quantum metric, which is the symmetric component of the QGT and takes complex values in non-Hermitian systems, generates an intrinsic nonlinear conduct…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115135] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026
item.dc:title: Non-Hermitian quantum geometric tensor and nonlinear electrical response
item.dc:creator: Kai Chen and Jie Zhu

item.title: Finite-size effects on metallization versus chiral Majorana fermions
item.description:
Author(s): Xin Yue (岳鑫), Guo-Jian Qiao (乔国健), and C. P. Sun (孙昌璞)

The search for chiral Majorana fermions in quantum anomalous Hall insulator/$s$-wave superconductor heterostructures has attracted intense interest, yet remains controversial due to the lack of conclusive evidence. A key issue is that the heterostructure's metallization can produce half-integer cond…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115416] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xin Yue (岳鑫), Guo-Jian Qiao (乔国健), and C. P. Sun (孙昌璞)

The search for chiral Majorana fermions in quantum anomalous Hall insulator/s-wave superconductor heterostructures has attracted intense interest, yet remains controversial due to the lack of conclusive evidence. A key issue is that the heterostructure's metallization can produce half-integer conduc…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115416] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026
item.dc:title: Finite-size effects on metallization versus chiral Majorana fermions

item.title: Ultrafast terahertz photoconductivity and near-field imaging of nanoscale inhomogeneities in multilayer epitaxial graphene nanoribbons
item.description:
Author(s): Arvind Singh, Jan Kunc, Tinkara Troha, Hynek Němec, and Petr Kužel

We study broadband terahertz (THz) conductivity and ultrafast photoconductivity spectra in lithographically fabricated multilayer epitaxial graphene nanoribbons grown on C-face of 6H-SiC substrate. THz near-field spectroscopy reveals local conductivity variations across nanoscale structural inhomoge…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115417] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Arvind Singh, Jan Kunc, Tinkara Troha, Hynek Němec, and Petr Kužel

We study broadband terahertz (THz) conductivity and ultrafast photoconductivity spectra in lithographically fabricated multilayer epitaxial graphene nanoribbons grown on C-face of 6H-SiC substrate. THz near-field spectroscopy reveals local conductivity variations across nanoscale structural inhomoge…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115417] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026
item.dc:title: Ultrafast terahertz photoconductivity and near-field imaging of nanoscale inhomogeneities in multilayer epitaxial graphene nanoribbons
item.dc:creator: Arvind Singh, Jan Kunc, Tinkara Troha, Hynek Němec, and Petr Kužel
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Microscopic phase-transition framework for gate-tunable superconductivity in monolayer ${\mathrm{WTe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): F. Yang, G. D. Zhao, Y. Shi, and L. Q. Chen

Gate-tunable superconductivity in monolayer WTe2 exhibits puzzling anomalies beyond the standard paradigm. Here, the authors develop a beyond-mean-field microscopic framework incorporating fermionic quasiparticles, bosonic Nambu-Goldstone phase modes, topological Berezinskii-Kosterlitz-Thouless excitations, and disorder to determine the superconducting gap, superfluid density, and phase transition. The theory explains the contrasting carrier-density dependence of the transition temperature in weakly and strongly disordered regimes and the sudden disappearance of superconducting fluctuations.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L100501] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): F. Yang, G. D. Zhao, Y. Shi, and L. Q. Chen

Gate-tunable superconductivity in monolayer WTe2 exhibits puzzling anomalies beyond the standard paradigm. Here, the authors develop a beyond-mean-field microscopic framework incorporating fermionic quasiparticles, bosonic Nambu-Goldstone phase modes, topological Berezinskii-Kosterlitz-Thouless excitations, and disorder to determine the superconducting gap, superfluid density, and phase transition. The theory explains the contrasting carrier-density dependence of the transition temperature in weakly and strongly disordered regimes and the sudden disappearance of superconducting fluctuations.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L100501] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026
item.dc:title: Microscopic phase-transition framework for gate-tunable superconductivity in monolayer ${\mathrm{WTe}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: F. Yang, G. D. Zhao, Y. Shi, and L. Q. Chen
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity
item.prism:section: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: Topological signatures of magnetic phase transitions with Majorana fermions through local observables and quantum information
item.description:
Author(s): Karyn Le Hur, Fan Yang, and Magali Korolev

The one-dimensional (1D) ${J}_{1}−{J}_{2}$ quantum spin model can be viewed as a strong-coupling analog of the Schrieffer-Su-Heeger model with two inequivalent alternating Ising couplings along the wire, associated to the physics of resonating valence bonds. Similar to the quantum Ising model, which…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L121106] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Karyn Le Hur, Fan Yang, and Magali Korolev

The one-dimensional (1D) J1−J2 quantum spin model can be viewed as a strong-coupling analog of the Schrieffer-Su-Heeger model with two inequivalent alternating Ising couplings along the wire, associated to the physics of resonating valence bonds. Similar to the quantum Ising model, which differently…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L121106] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026
item.dc:title: Topological signatures of magnetic phase transitions with Majorana fermions through local observables and quantum information
item.dc:creator: Karyn Le Hur, Fan Yang, and Magali Korolev

item.title: Roles of electron-magnon cross diffusion in unidirectional magnetoresistance of metallic magnetic bilayers
item.description:
Author(s): Shashank Gupta and Steven S.-L. Zhang

Unidirectional magnetoresistance (UMR) in metallic bilayers arises from nonlinear spin-charge transport mediated by broken time-reversal and inversion symmetries, yet the role of magnons remains unsettled. We develop a theoretical framework that incorporates coupled electron-magnon dynamics, reveali…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094434] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shashank Gupta and Steven S.-L. Zhang

Unidirectional magnetoresistance (UMR) in metallic bilayers arises from nonlinear spin-charge transport mediated by broken time-reversal and inversion symmetries, yet the role of magnons remains unsettled. We develop a theoretical framework that incorporates coupled electron-magnon dynamics, reveali…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094434] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Roles of electron-magnon cross diffusion in unidirectional magnetoresistance of metallic magnetic bilayers

item.title: Localization-landscape generalized Mott-Berezinskiĭ formula
item.description:
Author(s): Gabriel Hayoun, Ilya A. Gruzberg, and Marcel Filoche

We introduce a conceptual reformulation of the Mott-Berezinskiĭ (MB) theory of low-frequency ac conductivity in disordered systems based on localization-landscape theory. Instead of assuming uniform localization and fixed hopping distances, transport is described through an effective potential whose…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104202] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Gabriel Hayoun, Ilya A. Gruzberg, and Marcel Filoche

We introduce a conceptual reformulation of the Mott-Berezinskiĭ (MB) theory of low-frequency ac conductivity in disordered systems based on localization-landscape theory. Instead of assuming uniform localization and fixed hopping distances, transport is described through an effective potential whose…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104202] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026

item.title: General theory for the anomalous valley Hall effect in two-dimensional tetragonal crystals via static control
item.description:
Author(s): Xinyi Wang, Hongfu Zhou, Luyin Zhao, and Fujun Liu

The valley degree of freedom provides a promising route for functionalizing two-dimensional materials, yet a unified principle for realizing the anomalous valley Hall effect (AVHE) in tetragonal systems under static control has remained elusive. Here, we establish a general symmetry criterion for ac…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104429] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xinyi Wang, Hongfu Zhou, Luyin Zhao, and Fujun Liu

The valley degree of freedom provides a promising route for functionalizing two-dimensional materials, yet a unified principle for realizing the anomalous valley Hall effect (AVHE) in tetragonal systems under static control has remained elusive. Here, we establish a general symmetry criterion for ac…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104429] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.dc:title: General theory for the anomalous valley Hall effect in two-dimensional tetragonal crystals via static control

item.title: Impact of the honeycomb spin lattice on topological magnons and edge states in ferromagnetic two-dimensional skyrmion crystals
item.description:
Author(s): Doried Ghader and Bilal Jabakhanji

Magnons have been intensively studied in two-dimensional (2D) ferromagnetic (FM) skyrmion crystals (SkXs) stabilized on Bravais lattices, particularly triangular and square lattices. In these systems, topological edge states (TESs) have been reported in higher-energy magnon gaps, while the first mag…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104430] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Doried Ghader and Bilal Jabakhanji

Magnons have been intensively studied in two-dimensional (2D) ferromagnetic (FM) skyrmion crystals (SkXs) stabilized on Bravais lattices, particularly triangular and square lattices. In these systems, topological edge states (TESs) have been reported in higher-energy magnon gaps, while the first mag…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104430] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Impact of the honeycomb spin lattice on topological magnons and edge states in ferromagnetic two-dimensional skyrmion crystals

item.title: Thermally controlled flux avalanche dynamics in the bulk superconductor NbTi
item.description:
Author(s): I. Abaloszewa, V. V. Chabanenko, and A. Abaloszew

We report spatiotemporally resolved measurements of flux avalanche dynamics in bulk superconducting NbTi using high-speed magneto-optical imaging. Tracking individual avalanche events reveals propagation velocities of 15–25 m/s, which are orders of magnitude slower than the km/s speeds observed in t…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104511] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): I. Abaloszewa, V. V. Chabanenko, and A. Abaloszew

We report spatiotemporally resolved measurements of flux avalanche dynamics in bulk superconducting NbTi using high-speed magneto-optical imaging. Tracking individual avalanche events reveals propagation velocities of 15–25 m/s, which are orders of magnitude slower than the km/s speeds observed in t…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104511] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Thermally controlled flux avalanche dynamics in the bulk superconductor NbTi

item.title: Angular dependence and powder average of resonant inelastic x-ray scattering
item.description:
Author(s): Timothy G. Burrow, Myrtille O. J. Y. Hunault, Fabien Besnard, Amélie Juhin, and Christian Brouder

Resonant inelastic x-ray scattering (RIXS) is a synchrotron-based spectroscopy that has seen growing interest across a range of scientific disciplines beyond fundamental physics. The interpretation of experimental RIXS data requires theoretical calculations based on the Kramers–Heisenberg formula. H…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115131] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026

The interpretation of experimental RIXS data requires theoretical calculations based on the Kramers–Heisenberg formula. H…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115131] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026

item.title: Generalized Nagaoka ferromagnetism accompanied by flavor-selective Mott states in an $\mathrm{SU}(N)$ Fermi-Hubbard model
item.description:
Author(s): Juntaro Fujii, Kazuki Yamamoto, and Akihisa Koga

We study the ferromagnetic instability in an $\mathrm{SU}(N)$ Fermi-Hubbard model on the hypercubic lattice. Combining dynamical mean-field theory with continuous-time quantum Monte Carlo simulations, we find that, in the strong-coupling regime at low temperatures, ferromagnetically ordered (FM) sta…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115132] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Juntaro Fujii, Kazuki Yamamoto, and Akihisa Koga

We study the ferromagnetic instability in an SU(N) Fermi-Hubbard model on the hypercubic lattice. Combining dynamical mean-field theory with continuous-time quantum Monte Carlo simulations, we find that, in the strong-coupling regime at low temperatures, ferromagnetically ordered (FM) states develop…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115132] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026

item.title: Anisotropic scattering rates in strain-tuned ${\mathrm{Sr}}_{2}{\mathrm{RuO}}_{4}$
item.description:
Author(s): Ben Currie, David T. S. Perkins, Evgeny Kozik, Joseph J. Betouras, and Jörg Schmalian

Motivated by recent angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy (ARPES) experiments, we analyze the temperature, frequency, and momentum dependence of the single-particle scattering rate in a model of the $γ$-band of ${\mathrm{Sr}}_{2}{\mathrm{RuO}}_{4}$ under strain, with particular emphasis on the b…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115134] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ben Currie, David T. S. Perkins, Evgeny Kozik, Joseph J. Betouras, and Jörg Schmalian

Motivated by recent angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy (ARPES) experiments, we analyze the temperature, frequency, and momentum dependence of the single-particle scattering rate in a model of the γ-band of Sr2RuO4 under strain, with particular emphasis on the behavior near the Lifshitz transi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115134] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Anisotropic scattering rates in strain-tuned ${\mathrm{Sr}}_{2}{\mathrm{RuO}}_{4}$

item.title: Realization of condensed moiré exciton polaritons with tunable coherence in twisted photonic lattices at room temperature
item.description:
Author(s): Chunzi Xing, Yu Wang, Tobias Schneider, Xiaokun Zhai, Xinzheng Zhang, Haitao Dai, Xiao Wang, Anlian Pan, Zhenyu Xiong, Hao Wu, Yuan Ren, Stefan Schumacher, Xuekai Ma, and Tingge Gao

Moiré lattices attract significant attention in double-layer graphene and transition metal dichalcogenides (TMD) layer heterostructures as well as in photonic crystals due to the interesting exotic physics that emerges within these structures. In this work we report the strong coupling between excit…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115308] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chunzi Xing, Yu Wang, Tobias Schneider, Xiaokun Zhai, Xinzheng Zhang, Haitao Dai, Xiao Wang, Anlian Pan, Zhenyu Xiong, Hao Wu, Yuan Ren, Stefan Schumacher, Xuekai Ma, and Tingge Gao

Moiré lattices attract significant attention in double-layer graphene and transition metal dichalcogenides (TMD) layer heterostructures as well as in photonic crystals due to the interesting exotic physics that emerges within these structures. In this work we report the strong coupling between excit…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115308] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026

item.title: Impact of dimensionality on universality of quantum Hall transitions
item.description:
Author(s): Qiwei Wan and Yi Zhang

Regardless of the model and platform details, critical phenomena exhibit universal behaviors that are remarkably consistent across various experiments and theories, resulting in a significant scientific success of condensed matter physics. One widely known and commonly used example is the two-dimens…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125132] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Qiwei Wan and Yi Zhang

Regardless of the model and platform details, critical phenomena exhibit universal behaviors that are remarkably consistent across various experiments and theories, resulting in a significant scientific success of condensed matter physics. One widely known and commonly used example is the two-dimens…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125132] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026

item.title: Ferroelectric manipulation of spin splitting in graphene nanoribbon/multilayer elemental heterostructures
item.description:
Author(s): Wanping Shen, Jinbo Shen, Yuqiang Huang, Yangning Zhang, and Yunhao Lu

The unconventional π magnetism emerging at the edges of zigzag graphene nanoribbons (ZGNRs) has attracted considerable interest in carbon-based spintronics, motivating extensive efforts to understand and manipulate their spin distributions. Inspired by the recent discovery of electrically switchable…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125422] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026

Inspired by the recent discovery of electrically switchable…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125422] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026

item.title: Electronic transport properties of cove-type edged graphene nanoribbons
item.description:
Author(s): Hong-Zong Lu, Xiao-Feng Chen, Irfan Hussain Bhat, Ai-Min Guo, and Qing-Feng Sun

Cove-type edged graphene nanoribbons (CGNRs), constructed by removing several consecutive carbon atoms at both edges of each supercell in zigzag graphene nanoribbons, have attracted considerable interest due to recent advances in bottom-up synthesis and their intriguing quantum phenomena. However, t…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125423] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026

However, t…

item.title: Topological origin of Floquet thermalization in periodically driven many-body systems
item.description:
Author(s): Hao-Yue Qi, Yue Wu, and Wei Zheng

Floquet engineering is a powerful manipulation method in modern quantum technology. However, unwanted heating remains a major challenge of Floquet engineering, motivating extensive studies of Floquet thermalization. In this work, we investigate the thermalization of periodically driven many-body sys…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094307] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hao-Yue Qi, Yue Wu, and Wei Zheng

Floquet engineering is a powerful manipulation method in modern quantum technology. However, unwanted heating remains a major challenge of Floquet engineering, motivating extensive studies of Floquet thermalization. In this work, we investigate the thermalization of periodically driven many-body sys…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094307] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Topological origin of Floquet thermalization in periodically driven many-body systems

item.dc:subject: Dynamics, dynamical systems, lattice effects
item.prism:section: Dynamics, dynamical systems, lattice effects

item.title: Magnetic order in the van der Waals magnet ${\mathrm{VCl}}_{3}$
item.description:
Author(s): Zeyu Kao, Yiqing Hao, Yimeng Gu, Lixing Chen, Enkang Zhang, Hao Zhang, Fabio Orlandi, Pascal Manuel, Junfeng Wang, Chao Dong, Chuanying Xi, Zefeng Chen, Changsong Xu, and Jun Zhao

Here, the authors report the synthesis of high-quality VCl3​ single crystals, a van der Waals honeycomb magnet exhibiting a structural transition at 103.7 K. Using neutron diffraction, they resolve a prominent zigzag magnetic order below 21.8 K, characterized by a specific 𝐤=(0, 0.5, 1) propagation vector. This zigzag order phase and an observed magnetization plateau establish VCl3​ as a premier platform for investigating anisotropic magnetism and field-induced phase transitions in two-dimensional honeycomb lattices.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094428] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zeyu Kao, Yiqing Hao, Yimeng Gu, Lixing Chen, Enkang Zhang, Hao Zhang, Fabio Orlandi, Pascal Manuel, Junfeng Wang, Chao Dong, Chuanying Xi, Zefeng Chen, Changsong Xu, and Jun Zhao

Here, the authors report the synthesis of high-quality VCl3​ single crystals, a van der Waals honeycomb magnet exhibiting a structural transition at 103.7 K. Using neutron diffraction, they resolve a prominent zigzag magnetic order below 21.8 K, characterized by a specific 𝐤=(0, 0.5, 1) propagation vector. This zigzag order phase and an observed magnetization plateau establish VCl3​ as a premier platform for investigating anisotropic magnetism and field-induced phase transitions in two-dimensional honeycomb lattices.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094428] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Magnetic order in the van der Waals magnet ${\mathrm{VCl}}_{3}$
item.dc:creator: Zeyu Kao, Yiqing Hao, Yimeng Gu, Lixing Chen, Enkang Zhang, Hao Zhang, Fabio Orlandi, Pascal Manuel, Junfeng Wang, Chao Dong, Chuanying Xi, Zefeng Chen, Changsong Xu, and Jun Zhao

item.title: Electron spin resonance signatures of Dirac spinons in the Gd-substituted kagome antiferromagnet ${\mathrm{Gd}}_{0.015}{\mathrm{Y}}_{0.985}{\mathrm{Cu}}_{3}{(\mathrm{OD})}_{6.5}{\mathrm{Br}}_{2.5}$
item.description:
Author(s): T. Y. Kim, C. Koo, K. Yu. Povarov, U. Jena, P. Khunita, S. A. Zvyagin, J. Wosnitza, and K.-Y. Choi

External stimuli offer a route to realize exotic states with nontrivial excitations in highly frustrated kagome antiferromagnets. Here, 1.5% ${\mathrm{Gd}}^{3+}$ ions are substituted for ${\mathrm{Y}}^{3+}$ ions in ${\mathrm{YCu}}_{3}$(OD)${}_{6.5}{\mathrm{Br}}_{2.5}$ (abbreviated as ${\mathrm{YCu}}…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094429] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): T. Y. Kim, C. Koo, K. Yu. Povarov, U. Jena, P. Khunita, S. A. Zvyagin, J. Wosnitza, and K.-Y. Choi

External stimuli offer a route to realize exotic states with nontrivial excitations in highly frustrated kagome antiferromagnets. Here, 1.5% Gd3+ ions are substituted for Y3+ ions in YCu3(OD)6.5Br2.5 (abbreviated as YCu3), yielding Gd0.015Y0.985Cu3(OD)6.5Br2.5, where the Gd3+ ions act as a local pro…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094429] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Electron spin resonance signatures of Dirac spinons in the Gd-substituted kagome antiferromagnet ${\mathrm{Gd}}_{0.015}{\mathrm{Y}}_{0.985}{\mathrm{Cu}}_{3}{(\mathrm{OD})}_{6.5}{\mathrm{Br}}_{2.5}$
item.dc:creator: T. Y. Kim, C. Koo, K. Yu. Povarov, U. Jena, P. Khunita, S. A. Zvyagin, J. Wosnitza, and K.-Y. Choi

item.title: Magnetic field and pressure tuning of the heavy fermion antiferromagnet CePdIn
item.description:
Author(s): Bin Shen, Feng Du, Rui Li, Hang Su, Yasuyuki Shimura, Takahiro Onimaru, Kazunori Umeo, Xin Lu, Toshiro Takabatake, Michael Smidman, and Huiqiu Yuan

Frustrated Kondo lattices are ideal platforms for studying how both the Kondo effect and quantum fluctuations compete with the magnetic exchange interactions that drive magnetic ordering. Here, we investigate the effect of tuning the heavy-fermion compound CePdIn, which crystallizes in the geometric…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115130] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026

Frustrated Kondo lattices are ideal platforms for studying how both the Kondo effect and quantum fluctuations compete with the magnetic exchange interactions that drive magnetic ordering. Here, we investigate the effect of tuning the heavy-fermion compound CePdIn, which crystallizes in the geometric…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115130] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026

item.dc:creator: Bin Shen, Feng Du, Rui Li, Hang Su, Yasuyuki Shimura, Takahiro Onimaru, Kazunori Umeo, Xin Lu, Toshiro Takabatake, Michael Smidman, and Huiqiu Yuan
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Feynman paradox in a spherical axion insulator
item.description:
Author(s): Anastasiia Chyzhykova, Jeroen van den Brink, and Flavio S. Nogueira

The Feynman paradox highlights that static electromagnetic fields can store angular momentum. Here, the authors show that axion electrodynamics in a spherical topological insulator provides a topological realization of this effect. Varying the distance of a nearby charged probe induces Hall currents on the surface that convert electromagnetic angular momentum into mechanical rotation of the sphere, with a frequency derived from the topological response.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115414] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Anastasiia Chyzhykova, Jeroen van den Brink, and Flavio S. Nogueira

The Feynman paradox highlights that static electromagnetic fields can store angular momentum. Here, the authors show that axion electrodynamics in a spherical topological insulator provides a topological realization of this effect. Varying the distance of a nearby charged probe induces Hall currents on the surface that convert electromagnetic angular momentum into mechanical rotation of the sphere, with a frequency derived from the topological response.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115414] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Feynman paradox in a spherical axion insulator
item.dc:creator: Anastasiia Chyzhykova, Jeroen van den Brink, and Flavio S. Nogueira

item.title: Ultrafast nonlinear optical response of twisted bilayer graphene quantum dots
item.description:
Author(s): Krishna Rana Magar and Vadym Apalkov

We study theoretically the nonlinear optical response of twisted AA- and AB-stacked bilayer graphene quantum dots placed in the field of an ultrashort optical pulse. We consider the cases of both linearly and circularly polarized pulses, and we characterize the nonlinear optical response of the syst…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125420] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Krishna Rana Magar and Vadym Apalkov

We study theoretically the nonlinear optical response of twisted AA- and AB-stacked bilayer graphene quantum dots placed in the field of an ultrashort optical pulse. We consider the cases of both linearly and circularly polarized pulses, and we characterize the nonlinear optical response of the syst…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125420] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026

item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Floquet engineering magnetism and superconductivity in the square-lattice Hubbard model
item.description:
Author(s): Jan-Niklas Herre, Takuya Okugawa, Ammon Fischer, Christoph Karrasch, and Dante M. Kennes

The authors study here the interplay of magnetic order and superconductivity in the periodically driven square-lattice Hubbard model. In their Floquet random-phase approximation, circularly polarized light reshapes magnetic fluctuations inducing an antiferromagnetic-to-ferromagnetic transition close to a Floquet-Lifshitz transition. Near the antiferromagnetic phase, the light-dressed magnetic fluctuations stabilize topological d+id superconductivity at low driving frequencies, while high-frequency driving tunes pairing towards spin-triplet p wave. The work establishes a route to Floquet engineered topological superconductivity.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L121103] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jan-Niklas Herre, Takuya Okugawa, Ammon Fischer, Christoph Karrasch, and Dante M. Kennes

The authors study here the interplay of magnetic order and superconductivity in the periodically driven square-lattice Hubbard model. In their Floquet random-phase approximation, circularly polarized light reshapes magnetic fluctuations inducing an antiferromagnetic-to-ferromagnetic transition close to a Floquet-Lifshitz transition. Near the antiferromagnetic phase, the light-dressed magnetic fluctuations stabilize topological d+id superconductivity at low driving frequencies, while high-frequency driving tunes pairing towards spin-triplet p wave. The work establishes a route to Floquet engineered topological superconductivity.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L121103] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Floquet engineering magnetism and superconductivity in the square-lattice Hubbard model
item.dc:creator: Jan-Niklas Herre, Takuya Okugawa, Ammon Fischer, Christoph Karrasch, and Dante M. Kennes

item.title: Strain tunable anomalous Hall and Nernst conductivities in compensated ferrimagnetic ${\mathrm{Mn}}_{3}\mathrm{Al}$
item.description:
Author(s): Guihyun Han, Minkyu Park, and S. H. Rhim

The tunability of anomalous Hall and Nernst conductivities is investigated in the compensated ferrimagnet ${\mathrm{Mn}}_{3}\mathrm{Al}$ under isotropic strain $(η)$ and chemical potential variation using first-principles calculations. At a chemical potential of $μ=−0.3\phantom{\rule{0.28em}{0ex}}\m…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094425] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Guihyun Han, Minkyu Park, and S. H. Rhim

The tunability of anomalous Hall and Nernst conductivities is investigated in the compensated ferrimagnet Mn3Al under isotropic strain (η) and chemical potential variation using first-principles calculations. At a chemical potential of μ=−0.3eV, three distinct topological features—Weyl points, nodal…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094425] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Strain tunable anomalous Hall and Nernst conductivities in compensated ferrimagnetic ${\mathrm{Mn}}_{3}\mathrm{Al}$

item.title: Electric field control of spin-valley polarization and the spin Hall effect in altermagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Hongfu Zhou, Xinyi Wang, and Fujun Liu

Altermagnetism offers a unique potential for spintronic applications through its distinctive spin-split band structure and coupling to crystal symmetries. However, achieving electric-field control of spin and valley degrees of freedom remains a fundamental challenge. Here, we develop a spin-group th…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094427] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026

Altermagnetism offers a unique potential for spintronic applications through its distinctive spin-split band structure and coupling to crystal symmetries. However, achieving electric-field control of spin and valley degrees of freedom remains a fundamental challenge. Here, we develop a spin-group th…

item.title: Stress-induced martensitic transformation in epitaxial Ni-Mn-Ga thin films and its correlation to optical and magneto-optical properties
item.description:
Author(s): M. Makeš, J. Zázvorka, M. Hubert, P. Veřtát, M. Rameš, J. Zemen, O. Heczko, and M. Veis

The optical and magneto-optical properties of thin epitaxial Ni-Mn-Ga films, with thicknesses ranging from 8 to 160 nm, were investigated across the spectral range of 0.7–6.4 eV. The films were deposited by dc magnetron sputtering on MgO (100) substrates with a stress-mediating Cr buffer layer. Stru…

The films were deposited by dc magnetron sputtering on MgO (100) substrates with a stress-mediating Cr buffer layer. Stru…

item.description:

We investigate the ground-state nature of the honeycomb Kitaev-$\mathrm{Γ}$ model in the material-relevant parameter regime through a combination of analytics and classical and quantum simulations. We find the classical model is imprinted with a tangle of highly structured spin double helices. This …

We find the classical model is imprinted with a tangle of highly structured spin double helices. This helix tangl…

item.title: Universal inverted hysteresis induced by interfacial noncollinearity: From metallic exchange-spring bilayers to ferromagnet/topological insulator heterostructures

Author(s): Guodong Wei, Hanqing Gong, Ligang Bai, Hangtian Wang, Zhiqin Zhou, Weiran Xie, Hanyu Huang, Jin Zou, Weisheng Zhao, Yanxue Chen, and Tianxiao Nie

Inverted hysteresis loops (IHLs) exhibiting negative coercivity and remanence represent a counterintuitive phenomenon in magnetic systems, with origins remaining elusive. Here, we address this long-standing paradox through combined experimental and theoretical investigations of two prototypical hete…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104424] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026

Inverted hysteresis loops (IHLs) exhibiting negative coercivity and remanence represent a counterintuitive phenomenon in magnetic systems, with origins remaining elusive. Here, we address this long-standing paradox through combined experimental and theoretical investigations of two prototypical hete…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104424] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026

item.description:

We conducted small-angle neutron scattering (SANS) experiments on the ferromagnetic semimetal ${\mathrm{EuB}}_{6}$, where we observed first-hand evidence for the presence of magnetic polarons. We carried out SANS experiments over a large range of scattering vectors $|\stackrel{⃗}{\mathbf{q}}|$, rang…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115127] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Gabrielle Beaudin, Alexandre Désilets-Benoit, Andrea Daniele Bianchi, Robert Arnold, Stavros Samothrakitis, Nikola Anna Galvan Leos, Jorge L. Gavilano, Michel Kenzelmann, Kilian D. Stenning, Mark Laver, Simon Gerber, Michael Nicklas, Robert Cubitt, and Charles Dewhurst

We conducted small-angle neutron scattering (SANS) experiments on the ferromagnetic semimetal EuB6, where we observed first-hand evidence for the presence of magnetic polarons. We carried out SANS experiments over a large range of scattering vectors |q⃗|, ranging from 0.006 to 0.140 Å−1 , and temper…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115127] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026

item.title: Designing second-order topological insulators in heterobilayers via interlayer coupling and out-of-plane magnetic fields
item.description:
Author(s): Xiaoliang Xiao, Weixiang Kong, Jiali Yang, Jin-Zhu Zhao, and Yuanjun Jin

The Zeeman effect, induced by the magnetic field, which can break the Dirac edge states of first-order topological insulators to generate a nontrivial gap, is a physical mechanism for the realization of two-dimensional second-order topological insulators (SOTIs). However, such magnetic-field-driven …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115128] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026

However, such magnetic-field-driven …

It is shown that a band gap is introduced by tuning the gate voltage, which modifies the pseudospin polarization and breaks anti-Klein tunneling at normal incidence. Specifically, when the pseud…

In one spatial dimension, we construct a frustration-free lattice model exhibiting SSB of a non-onsite symmetry, which features the coexistence of two ground states with …

In one spatial dimension, we construct a frustration-free lattice model exhibiting SSB of a non-onsite symmetry, which features the coexistence of two ground states with distinct symmetr…

item.title: Quantum phase diagram of the extended spin-$\frac{3}{2}$ Kitaev-Heisenberg model: A DMRG study

Recently, there has been considerable excitement surrounding the promising realization of high-spin Kitaev material, such as the quasi-2D compound ${\mathrm{CrI}}_{3}$ and ${\mathrm{CrGeTe}}_{3}$. However, the stability of quantum spin liquids (QSL) against single-ion anisotropy (SIA) in these mater…

Author(s): Gui-Xin Liu, Ting-Long Wang, and Yi-Fan Jiang

Recently, there has been considerable excitement surrounding the promising realization of high-spin Kitaev material, such as the quasi-2D compound CrI3 and CrGeTe3. However, the stability of quantum spin liquids (QSL) against single-ion anisotropy (SIA) in these materials and the global quantum phas…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125125] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026

item.title: Experimental confirmation of the magnetic ordering transition induced by an electronic structure change in the metallic triangular antiferromagnet ${\mathrm{Co}}_{1/3}{\mathrm{TaS}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Han-Jin Noh, En-Jin Cho, Byeong-Gyu Park, Hyowon Park, Ivar Martin, Cristian D. Batista, Pyeongjae Park, Woonghee Cho, and Je-Geun Park

We report angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy (ARPES) studies combined with $\mathrm{DFT}+\mathrm{DMFT}$ calculations to confirm that the magnetic ordering vector transition from $\mathrm{Q}=(1/2,0,0)$ to $\mathrm{Q}=(1/3,0,0)$ in the metallic triangular antiferromagnets ${\mathrm{Co}}_{1/3±ε}…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125126] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Han-Jin Noh, En-Jin Cho, Byeong-Gyu Park, Hyowon Park, Ivar Martin, Cristian D. Batista, Pyeongjae Park, Woonghee Cho, and Je-Geun Park

We report angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy (ARPES) studies combined with DFT+DMFT calculations to confirm that the magnetic ordering vector transition from Q=(1/2,0,0) to Q=(1/3,0,0) in the metallic triangular antiferromagnets Co1/3±εTaS2 (ε≈0.007) is induced by the electronic structure cha…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125126] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Experimental confirmation of the magnetic ordering transition induced by an electronic structure change in the metallic triangular antiferromagnet ${\mathrm{Co}}_{1/3}{\mathrm{TaS}}_{2}$

We investigate electron pairing in a super clean kagome superconductor ${\mathrm{CsV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ with a residual resistivity ratio (RRR) of 290. Using the dilution-refrigerator-based scanning tunneling microscopy (STM) at the Synergetic Extreme Condition User Facility (SECUF), we find th…

item.content:encoded:

We investigate electron pairing in a super clean kagome superconductor CsV3Sb5 with a residual resistivity ratio (RRR) of 290. Using the dilution-refrigerator-based scanning tunneling microscopy (STM) at the Synergetic Extreme Condition User Facility (SECUF), we find that the pairing gap exhibits ch…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125127] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026
